title: Chapter 8 Quiz: Control Flow
description: Test your understanding of conditionals and loops!

questions.explanation: x > 5 is TRUE, so the if block executes, printing 'yes'.

questions.question: The condition in an if statement can be a logical vector of length > 1.
questions.explanation: if requires a single logical value; use any() or all() to reduce a vector.

questions.question: The vectorized conditional function is `_____`.
questions.explanation: ifelse() works element-wise on vectors.

questions.question: What does `ifelse(c(TRUE, FALSE), 10, 20)` return?
questions.explanation: TRUE -> 10, FALSE -> 20, so c(10,20).
It iterates over 1,2,3 – three times.

questions.question: A while loop always runs at least once.
questions.explanation: If the condition is initially FALSE, the loop body never runs.

questions.question: To exit a loop immediately, use `_____`.
questions.explanation: break terminates the loop.
When i=3, next skips the print, so output: 1,2,4.
x starts at 5, prints, becomes 3, prints, becomes 1, prints, becomes -1, stops because condition x>0 is FALSE.

questions.question: `repeat` loops must contain a `break` to avoid infinite execution.
questions.explanation: repeat is infinite unless break is used.

questions.question: In an if-else chain, the `else` must be on the same line as the closing brace of the previous `if` in R.
questions.explanation: Yes, else must follow the closing brace on the same line (or use braces appropriately).

questions.question: What is the result of `ifelse(1:5 > 3, "high", "low")`?
questions.explanation: 1:5 > 3 gives F,F,F,T,T, so low,low,low,high,high.

questions.question: Nested ifelse statements can replace multiple if-else blocks for vector inputs.
questions.explanation: Yes, but they can become hard to read.
Outer loop runs 3 times, inner 2 times, total 6.

questions.question: The `next` statement is used to skip the rest of the current _____.
questions.explanation: next jumps to the next iteration.
x accumulates 1+2+3+4 = 10.

questions.question: A for loop can iterate over a character vector.
questions.explanation: Yes, for loops work on any vector.

questions.question: Which statement immediately terminates a loop?
questions.explanation: break exits the loop.

questions.question: To check if any element of a logical vector is TRUE, use `_____`.
questions.explanation: any() returns TRUE if at least one element is TRUE.
First if is FALSE, second condition TRUE, so 'B' is printed.
